title: Foreign Key Cache
text: Foreign Key Cache When the one entity is nested inside the many entity, Uniface uses a foreign key cache when retrieving the data. Figure 1. Foreign entity Department is a foreign entity: Before generating a Select on a foreign key when fetching an occurrence of the many entity into the component, Uniface first checks the foreign key cache to see if the data for the foreign entity is already present in the component. This can greatly improve performance if the user retrieves multiple occurrences of the outermost entity for which the foreign key does not change. For example, if you retrieve PERSON occurrences with a foreign key link to DEPARTMENT, for people located in the same department as the previously fetched one, the Select for the DEPARTMENT data comes from the foreign key cache, instead of from the database. A Select operation from cache is much faster than going to the database. The foreign key cache is completely automatic. Related Links Format of Overflow Tables Uniface overflow tables are in a format that only Uniface can understand. If you use a non-Uniface product to access the data, the product must be configured to understand overflow tables. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
